title: „Soluția imorală”: ce a fost și ce nu mai e
summary: The article analyzes how Romanian presidents have historically positioned themselves when forced into coalitions with political adversaries, coining it 'the immoral solution.' It critiques current President Nicușor Dan for failing to maintain political independence from PSD, contrasting his passive neutrality with Băsescu's transparent self-criticism and Iohannis's initial anti-PSD stance.
talking_points: Băsescu coined the 'immoral solution' in 2004 to transparently acknowledge his uncomfortable coalition with PUR/Dan Voiculescu's party, maintaining voter trust through honesty
Iohannis initially adopted a confrontational anti-PSD stance but eventually capitulated through the 'governmental rotary' power-sharing arrangement
President Nicușor Dan failed to support his own Foreign Minister Oana Țoiu in the Dubai repatriation scandal involving ex-PM Ponta
Dan's condescending treatment of Cotroceni protesters opposing PSD justice minister nominations suggests alignment with PSD rather than neutrality
Romanian political culture is built on confrontation rather than rational compromise, making neutrality a risky bet that can alienate supporters without winning opponents
author: Cristian Preda
